mission.title: harbor: Proxy cache does not provide images when target registry is not working
mission.description: Proxy cache does not provide images when target registry is not working. This issue affects 15+ users.

steps.title: Identify harbor troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your harbor deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n harbor --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review harbor configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant harbor configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl get configmap -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=harbor
```
The Proxy cache is one of the most important features of harbor to ensure that the clients will get the most recent available images without having to connect to the upstream registry.

steps.title: Upgrade Harbor for improved proxy cache fallback
steps.description:
Upgrade Harbor to a version with improved proxy cache fallback behavior:
```bash
# Update the Harbor Helm repo
helm repo update harbor

# Upgrade Harbor
helm upgrade harbor harbor/harbor -n harbor -f values.yaml

# Verify pods restart successfully
kubectl rollout status deployment/harbor-core -n harbor
```
After upgrading, the proxy cache will serve cached images even when the upstream registry is temporarily unavailable.

steps.title: Confirm Proxy cache does not provide images when target… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n harbor --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n harbor --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: When the upstream registry goes down, Harbor's proxy cache does not serve locally cached images because the health check interval (5 minutes) may not have detected the outage yet. If a pull request arrives before the registry status is marked unhealthy, the proxy cache attempts to reach the upstream and fails instead of falling back to cached images. The fix improves fallback behavior to serve cached images when the upstream is unreachable, regardless of the health check status.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with harbor installed or the issue environment reproducible.
